description:
Enabling cross-platform application and game development

HARMAN have taken over the development and support for the Adobe AIR SDK, a cross-platform framework used by a wide range of applications and games. Enabling rapid development including animations created via Adobe Animate, AIR supports the deployment of applications on desktop and mobile platforms, including the ability to develop and deploy iPhone and iPad applications using a Windows machine.
AIR-4887: AIR package format needs to allow code-signed MacOS binaries
AIR-7993: ADT on macOS to allow IPA default values rather than using xcode
Github-4210: Updating build and packaging to use universal binary on ios-simulator
Github-4242: Removing UILaunchImages in an IPA if there is a storyboard
AIR-7992: Do not delete a license file if network failures prevent validation
AIR-7996: Android mediacodec/videotexture usage gives a JNI error
AIR-7999: Ensuring iOS ANEs can specify minimum iOS version in sdkVersion field
AIR-8004: Ensure ADT doesn't use old locations for license files
Github-1803: Ensuring symbolic links in ANEs are supported in Linux bundle packages
Github-1870: Ensuring the Windows runtime does not hang at the splash screen
Github-4157: Ensuring Android camera list works with logical multi-cameras
Github-4216: Removing libysshared.so from Android runtime.apk files
Github-4221: AIR Windows stability improvements in audio handling
Github-4224: Fixing deadlock in iOS on parallel URL downloads
Github-4225: Ensuring ADT prevents Android strings.xml values from overwriting AIR values
Github-4234: Fixing typo in config file, and updating based on latest supported flags
Github-4236: Ensuring gcEfficiency minimum value (0.01) can be used
Github-4240: Fixing stability issue when running print job from StageWebView callback
Github-4241: Ensuring ADT rename option works for root SWF in IPA files
